description: Fix two CLI UX issues discovered during fresh-user testing: the `update` command shows a Node.js deprecation warning (DEP0190), and `config set tool-permission` silently accepts nonexistent tool names.

userStories.title: Remove shell:true from spawnSync calls in update command to fix DEP0190 warning
userStories.description: Remove `shell: true` from two `spawnSync` calls in the update command that trigger Node.js DEP0190 deprecation warning on Node 24+.
acceptanceCriteria: Running `opentabs update` on Node 24+ produces no DEP0190 deprecation warning on stderr
`opentabs update` still correctly reports 'Already up to date' when current version matches latest
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/cli/src/commands/update.ts (two spawnSync calls to fix)

Context:
On Node.js 24+, passing `shell: true` with an args array to `spawnSync` triggers DEP0190: "Passing args to a child process with shell option true can lead to security vulnerabilities, as the arguments are not escaped, only concatenated." Users see this on every `opentabs update` run.

Changes:
1. In `getLatestVersion()` (line 35), remove `shell: true`:
   Current: `const result = spawnSync('npm', ['view', CLI_PACKAGE_NAME, 'version'], { stdio: ['ignore', 'pipe', 'pipe'], shell: true });`
   Change to: `const result = spawnSync('npm', ['view', CLI_PACKAGE_NAME, 'version'], { stdio: ['ignore', 'pipe', 'pipe'] });`

2. In `performUpdate()` (line 176), remove `shell: true`:
   Current: `const result = spawnSync('npm', ['install', '-g', target], { stdio: 'inherit', shell: true });`
   Change to: `const result = spawnSync('npm', ['install', '-g', target], { stdio: 'inherit' });`

Constraints:
- Do NOT change any other spawnSync calls in the file (e.g., the one at line 145 in `restartBackgroundServer` which correctly does NOT use `shell: true`)
- The `spawnSync` calls with a string command and args array work correctly without `shell: true` — Node resolves `npm` from PATH automatically

userStories.title: Warn when config set tool-permission targets a nonexistent tool
userStories.description: Add validation to `handleSetToolPermission` that warns users when the specified tool name does not exist in the running server's tool registry, preventing typos from silently writing useless config entries.
acceptanceCriteria: Setting a permission for a nonexistent tool prints a yellow warning like 'Warning: tool "nonexistent_tool" not found in plugin "browser". The permission was saved but may have no effect.'
Setting a permission for an existing tool (e.g., `tool-permission.browser.browser_execute_script off`) does NOT show any warning
When the server is not running, the permission is saved without validation (no warning about the tool, since tools cannot be checked offline)
The permission is still written to config even when the warning is shown (user may be pre-configuring for a plugin they haven't installed yet)
